1968 (1968) (as Centre Universitaire Dauphine)  1970 (as Université Paris IX Dauphine) 2004 (as Université Paris-Dauphine)

[Chancellery of the Universities of Paris](/wiki/Chancellerie_des_Universit%C3%A9s_de_Paris "Chancellerie des Universités de Paris") [Conférence des grandes écoles](/wiki/Conf%C3%A9rence_des_grandes_%C3%A9coles "Conférence des grandes écoles"), [Couperin](/wiki/Couperin_(consortium) "Couperin (consortium)") consortium,[[3]](#cite_note-3) [EQUIS](/wiki/EQUIS "EQUIS")

This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Paris_Dauphine_University "Special:EditPage/Paris Dauphine University") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Paris Dauphine University"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Université Paris Dauphine - PSL*) is a [Grande École](/wiki/Grande_%C3%89cole "Grande École") and public institution of higher education and research based in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [France](/wiki/France "France"), [constituent college](/wiki/Collegiate_university "Collegiate university") of [PSL University](/wiki/PSL_University "PSL University"). As of 2022, Dauphine has 9,400 students in 8 fields of study (law, economics, finance, computer science, journalism, management, mathematics, social sciences), plus 3,800 in executive education.[[1]](#cite_note-annual-report-2022-1)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Its status as a *[grand établissement](/wiki/Grands_%C3%A9tablissements "Grands établissements")*,[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) adopted in 2004, allows it to select its students.[[6]](#cite_note-6) On average, 90 to 95% of accepted students received either high distinctions or the highest distinctions at their French High School National Exam results (Examen National du Baccalauréat).[[7]](#cite_note-7) Dauphine is also a member of the [Conférence des Grandes Écoles](/wiki/Conf%C3%A9rence_des_Grandes_%C3%A9coles "Conférence des Grandes écoles").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Paris+Dauphine+University%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Paris Dauphine University - PSL** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Université Paris Dauphine - PSL*) is a [Grande École](/wiki/Grande_%C3%89cole "Grande École") and public institution of higher education and research based in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [France](/wiki/France "France"), [constituent college](/wiki/Collegiate_university "Collegiate university") of [PSL University](/wiki/PSL_University "PSL University"). As of 2022, Dauphine has 9,400 students in 8 fields of study (law, economics, finance, computer science, journalism, management, mathematics, social sciences), plus 3,800 in executive education.[[1]](#cite_note-annual-report-2022-1)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Its status as a *[grand établissement](/wiki/Grands_%C3%A9tablissements "Grands établissements")*,[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) adopted in 2004, allows it to select its students.[[6]](#cite_note-6) On average, 90 to 95% of accepted students received either high distinctions or the highest distinctions at their French High School National Exam results (Examen National du Baccalauréat).[[7]](#cite_note-7) Dauphine is also a member of the [Conférence des Grandes Écoles](/wiki/Conf%C3%A9rence_des_Grandes_%C3%A9coles "Conférence des Grandes écoles").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Research at Dauphine concerns "organization and decision sciences", organized in 6 research laboratories (5 of which are mixed units also staffed by [CNRS](/wiki/French_National_Centre_for_Scientific_Research "French National Centre for Scientific Research") researchers): the *CEREMADE* Center for Research in Decision Mathematics, the *CR2D* Dauphine Law Research Center, *DRM* Dauphine Management Research, the *IRISSO* Interdisciplinary Research Institute in Social Science, the *LAMSADE* Laboratory for Analysis and Modeling of Decision Support Systems, and the *LEDa* Dauphine Economics Laboratory. A total of 519 research staff work at Dauphine.[[1]](#cite_note-annual-report-2022-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paris_Dauphine_University&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Dauphine was founded on 24 October 1968 as a university center with the status of a faculty, named *Centre universitaire Dauphine*.[[9]](#cite_note-9) On 17 December 1970, as part of the division of the ancient [University of Paris](/wiki/University_of_Paris "University of Paris") into 13 universities, it became an "[établissement public à caractère scientifique et culturel](/wiki/Public_Scientific_and_Technical_Research_Establishment "Public Scientific and Technical Research Establishment")", named *Université Paris-IX Dauphine*.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

The university was established in the Palais Dauphine, also known as the Palais de l'OTAN ("[NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") Palace"), a building designed by [Jacques Carlu](/wiki/Jacques_Carlu "Jacques Carlu") and built at Porte Dauphine between 1955 and 1957 to serve as the [NATO headquarters](/wiki/NATO_headquarters "NATO headquarters"). It served that function between 1959 and 1966, when France left the NATO military structure.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Paris-IX Dauphine was designated as an "experimental university", and was one of the very few universities in France to select students on the basis of their [Baccalauréat](/wiki/Baccalaur%C3%A9at "Baccalauréat") scores. The legality of this was disputed, and some rejected students threatened lawsuits and were subsequently quietly admitted, a strategy that became increasingly popular by 2002.[[12]](#cite_note-12) In response, in 2004, Dauphine ceased being a public university and became a *[grand établissement](/wiki/Grands_%C3%A9tablissements "Grands établissements")* under the name *Université Paris-Dauphine,*[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) which legally allows it to practice selective admissions.

In 2009, Université Paris-Dauphine gained [EQUIS](/wiki/EFMD_Quality_Improvement_System "EFMD Quality Improvement System") ([EFMD](/wiki/European_Foundation_for_Management_Development "European Foundation for Management Development") Quality Improvement System) accreditation, awarded by the [European Foundation for Management Development](/wiki/European_Foundation_for_Management_Development "European Foundation for Management Development"). It is a founding member of the [Executive DBA Council](/wiki/Executive_DBA_Council "Executive DBA Council").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 2011, Université Paris-Dauphine was one of the 16 co-founders of [Paris Sciences et Lettres University](/wiki/Paris_Sciences_et_Lettres_University "Paris Sciences et Lettres University") (PSL). On 5 November 2019, PSL became formally established as a public university, organized in the form of a [collegiate university](/wiki/Collegiate_university "Collegiate university") (modelled after British collegiate universities such as Oxford and Cambridge) allowing its constituent institutions to keep their legal personality.[[14]](#cite_note-14) On the same date, Dauphine officially became a college of PSL.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) It now self-styles its name as *Université Paris Dauphine - PSL*.

## International degree offers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paris_Dauphine_University&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International degree offers")]

Paris Dauphine University - PSL has a campus in [Tunis](/wiki/Tunis "Tunis") offering bachelors and masters programs,[[15]](#cite_note-15) and a campus in London offering courses in cooperation with [University College London](/wiki/University_College_London "University College London").[[16]](#cite_note-16)

It offers joint degree programs with the [Autonomous University of Madrid](/wiki/Autonomous_University_of_Madrid "Autonomous University of Madrid") (in economics, management, and social sciences),[[17]](#cite_note-17) and with [Goethe University](/wiki/Johann_Wolfgang_Goethe_University_of_Frankfurt_am_Main "Johann Wolfgang Goethe University of Frankfurt am Main") in Frankfurt (in economic sciences).[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Rankings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paris_Dauphine_University&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Rankings")]

* 2025: As a part of [Université PSL](/wiki/PSL_Research_University "PSL Research University"), Dauphine is ranked the 24th-best university in the world according to the Times Higher Education World University Rankings[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2025: As a part of [Université PSL](/wiki/PSL_Research_University "PSL Research University"), Dauphine is ranked 24th-best university in the world according to the QS World University Rankings[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* 2024: As a part of [Université PSL](/wiki/PSL_Research_University "PSL Research University"), Dauphine is ranked 19th-best university in the world according to the [Center for World University Rankings](/wiki/Center_for_World_University_Rankings "Center for World University Rankings")[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* 2023: As a part of [Université PSL](/wiki/PSL_Research_University "PSL Research University"), Dauphine is ranked as the 3rd-best young university in the world according to Times Higher Education World University Rankings[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* 2020: As a part of [Université PSL](/wiki/PSL_Research_University "PSL Research University"), Dauphine is ranked 36th-best university in the world according to the Shanghai ranking[[23]](#cite_note-23)
